sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Customer.io connector** integrates with Customer.io via the App API v1. It enables Shielva to sync customers, campaigns, and newsletters from your Customer.io workspace into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **App API Key** authentication — a Bearer token sent as `Authorization: Bearer {app_api_key}` on every request.

**Key capabilities:**
- Validate the API key via `GET /accounts` on install and health check
- Sync all customers with cursor-based pagination (handles workspaces of any size)
- Sync automated campaigns, newsletter sends, and segments
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256('customer:' + id)[:16] for customers and SHA-256(id)[:16] for campaigns/newsletters
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off for transient errors and rate limits
- Circuit breaker (threshold: 5 failures, 60s recovery) for health degradation detection

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `customerio` — registered in ACP as **Customer.io**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Customer.io (App API v1 — `https://api.customer.io/v1/`)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates app_api_key and calls GET /accounts; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE with reason |
| `health_check()` | Calls GET /accounts to verify key; returns workspace name in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches all customers (cursor-paginated) + campaigns + newsletters; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_customers(start, limit)` | Cursor-paginated POST /customers search |
| `get_customer(customer_id)` | GET /customers/{customer_id} for a single customer |
| `list_campaigns(page, limit)` | GET /campaigns — automated campaigns |
| `get_campaign(campaign_id)` | GET /campaigns/{campaign_id} for a single campaign |
| `list_newsletters(page, limit)` | GET /newsletters — one-off newsletter sends |
| `list_segments(page, limit)` | GET /segments — audience segments |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector uses **App API Key** authentication. The key is sent as `Authorization: Bearer {app_api_key}` on every request.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `api_key` — create an App API key in Customer.io at Account Settings → API Credentials. Use the **App API key**, not the Track API key.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| App API Key | `app_api_key` | password | Yes | Customer.io App API key from Account Settings → API Credentials |

children.title: Key Validation
children.content: The connector validates the key by calling `GET /accounts`. A 401/403 response returns INVALID_CREDENTIALS. Any 5xx or network error returns FAILED.

children.title: Key Security
children.content: App API keys can be revoked at any time from Customer.io → Account Settings → API Credentials. Shielva stores the key AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest via the vault.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `app_api_key` | Customer.io App API key |

children.title: Request Headers
children.content:
Every request includes:
```
Authorization: Bearer {app_api_key}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `CustomerIOHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Validates the App API key and establishes the connector.

**Steps:**
1. Check `app_api_key` is non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
2. Call `GET /accounts` via `with_retry`.
3. Extract `account.name` from response.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Customer.io — Acme Corp')`
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` — no key
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` — 401/403
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` — network/server error

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Pings `GET /accounts` and returns health with workspace name.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Customer.io — Acme Corp')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on 401/403
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error (circuit breaker not open)
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error (circuit breaker open)

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Full sync across three resource types:
1. **Customers** — cursor-paginated via the `next` field in `POST /customers` responses, normalized via `normalize_customer()`
2. **Campaigns** — page/limit pagination via `GET /campaigns`, normalized via `normalize_campaign()`
3. **Newsletters** — page/limit pagination via `GET /newsletters`, normalized to ConnectorDocuments

Campaign and newsletter failures are non-fatal — customer sync is the primary resource.

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed.
Status: `COMPLETED` when no failures; `PARTIAL` when some normalizations fail; `FAILED` when customer API call fails.

children.title: list_customers()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_customers(self, start=None, limit=100) -> dict`

Single-page call to `POST /customers`. For multi-page iteration, call repeatedly with the cursor from the `next` field.

**Parameters:**
- `start` — opaque cursor string from previous page's `next` field
- `limit` — records per page (default 100)

**Returns:** Response with `customers` list and `next` cursor (or None).

children.title: get_customer()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_customer(self, customer_id: str) -> dict`

Fetches a single customer via `GET /customers/{customer_id}`.

**Raises:** `CustomerIONotFoundError` if the customer does not exist.

children.title: list_campaigns()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_campaigns(self, page=1, limit=50) -> dict`

Fetches automated campaigns via `GET /campaigns`.

**Returns:** Response with `campaigns` list.

children.title: get_campaign()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_campaign(self, campaign_id) -> dict`

Fetches a single campaign via `GET /campaigns/{campaign_id}`.

children.title: list_newsletters()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_newsletters(self, page=1, limit=50) -> dict`

Fetches newsletters via `GET /newsletters`.

**Returns:** Response with `newsletters` list.

children.title: list_segments()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_segments(self, page=1, limit=50) -> dict`

Fetches audience segments via `GET /segments`.

**Returns:** Response with `segments` list.

sections.title: HTTP Client
sections.content: All HTTP calls are encapsulated in `CustomerIOHTTPClient` (`client/http_client.py`) using `httpx.AsyncClient`. The connector never calls HTTP directly.

children.title: Methods
children.content:
| Method | Customer.io API Call | Description |
|--------|---------------------|-------------|
| `get_account()` | `GET /accounts` | Validate key; returns account info |
| `search_customers(start, limit)` | `POST /customers` | Customers with cursor pagination |
| `get_customer(customer_id)` | `GET /customers/{id}` | Single customer |
| `list_campaigns(page, limit)` | `GET /campaigns` | Automated campaigns |
| `get_campaign(campaign_id)` | `GET /campaigns/{id}` | Single campaign |
| `list_newsletters(page, limit)` | `GET /newsletters` | Newsletter sends |
| `list_segments(page, limit)` | `GET /segments` | Audience segments |

children.title: Pagination
children.content:
Customer.io uses two pagination styles:

**Cursor-based** (customers via POST /customers):
```json
{
  "customers": [...],
  "next": "opaque_cursor_value"
}
```
The connector passes `next` as `start` in the next request until `next` is null/absent.

**Page-based** (campaigns, newsletters, segments):
```
GET /campaigns?page=1&limit=50
```

children.title: Error Mapping
children.content:
HTTP status codes are mapped to typed exceptions:

| HTTP Status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401 | `CustomerIOAuthError` |
| 403 | `CustomerIOAuthError` |
| 404 | `CustomerIONotFoundError` |
| 429 | `CustomerIORateLimitError` (with retry_after) |
| Connection/timeout | `CustomerIONetworkError` |
| 5xx | `CustomerIOServerError` |
| Other 4xx | `CustomerIOError` |

children.title: Retry Logic
children.content:
`with_retry()` in `helpers/utils.py` wraps API calls with exponential back-off:
- `max_attempts=3` (configurable)
- Back-off: base 1s × 2^attempt + random jitter (0–0.5s), capped at 30s
- Skips retry on `CustomerIOAuthError` — re-installing with a valid key is required
- For `CustomerIORateLimitError`: honours `Retry-After` header if > 0, otherwise uses back-off formula

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Normalized documents produced by `normalize_customer()` and `normalize_campaign()` in `helpers/utils.py`, and `_normalize_newsletter()` in `connector.py`.

children.title: Customer ConnectorDocument
Campaign ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256(campaign_id)[:16]>",
  "title": "Customer.io campaign: Onboarding Series",
  "content": "Campaign ID: 101\nName: Onboarding Series\nStatus: active\nMessage type: email\nTags: onboarding, automated",
  "source_url": "https://fly.customer.io/env/0/campaigns/101",
  "metadata": {
    "campaign_id": 101,
    "name": "Onboarding Series",
    "status": "active",
    "msg_type": "email",
    "tags": ["onboarding", "automated"]
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content:
The `source_id` field is computed as:
- **Customers:** `SHA-256('customer:' + customer_id)[:16]` — the 'customer:' prefix ensures customer IDs don't collide with campaign/newsletter IDs in the document namespace.
- **Campaigns/Newsletters:** `SHA-256(str(id))[:16]`

This is stable across syncs — the same Customer.io customer or campaign always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-ID lists.

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `CustomerIOError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
CustomerIOError (base)
├── CustomerIOAuthError           — 401, 403 responses
│   └── CustomerIOInvalidKeyError — key format invalid
├── CustomerIONetworkError        — connectivity failures, timeouts
├── CustomerIORateLimitError      — 429 Too Many Requests (has retry_after attr)
├── CustomerIONotFoundError       — 404 Not Found
└── CustomerIOServerError         — 5xx responses
```

Callers can catch the base `CustomerIOError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `app_api_key is required` | No key provided | Enter the App API key in the install form |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Invalid or revoked key | Regenerate the key in Customer.io → Account Settings → API Credentials |
| `403 Forbidden` | Using Track API key | Use the **App** API key, not the Track API key |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Rate limit exceeded | Connector retries automatically with exponential back-off |
| No customers returned | Empty workspace | Verify the workspace has customers and the key is correct |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (67 tests, all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/customerio_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 67 tests must pass before registering the connector.
